text: The surgical team attempts to dock the robotic arm for the liver metastasis resection but encounters a spatial constraint between the costal margin and anterior superior iliac spine due to the patient's positioning. The team then switches to a laparoscopic approach.

The surgeon performs an intraoperative ultrasound to assess the liver metastasis, followed by an oncological resection leaving a margin of at least one centimeter from the lesion. The resected specimen is placed inside a retrieval bag for delayed extraction.

The patient is repositioned for the second robotic docking, with the robot approaching from the right upper quadrant. Initially, the surgeon performs a laparoscopic incision of the gastrocolic ligament up to the right colon angle, marking the transverse colon resection site. After docking the robot, dissection proceeds from middle to lateral, identifying the vascular plane between the Gerota fascia and Toldt fascia. The surgeon isolates and sections the ileocecal colonic vein and artery between Hem-o-lok clips. The right mesocolon is transected in a corticranial direction along the lateral margin of the superior mesenteric axis. The right colic vessels and the right branch of the middle colic pedicles are sectioned at their origin.

The surgeon uses a mechanical stapler to section the transverse colon and the lascilia loops. An intracorporeal lateral-lateral isoperistaltic anastomosis is performed with the stapler, followed by closure of the enterotomies using a double-layer suture technique: first with a running suture of PDS-III-O, then with interrupted sutures of II-O Vicryl. The mesenteric defect is left open.

The surgeon begins the third phase of the procedure by incising the parietal peritoneum at the base of the sigmoid mesentery using laparoscopy, proceeding medially to laterally between the Toldt and Gerota fascia until reaching the rectum. The robotic cart is docked from the left leg, and posterior dissection is performed between the fascia recti and Waldeyer's fascia down to the pelvic floor for total mesorectal excision. After visualizing the lateral ligaments bilaterally, anterior dissection is carried out along the rectovaginal septum, completing the abdominal portion. The sigmoid colon is then sectioned with a mechanical stapler, transitioning to perineal surgery.
The surgeon removes the surgical specimen and extracts the right colon and liver metastasis through the perineal incision. The final view shows the patient's abdomen post-procedure.
